title: List Basics Practice
instructions: Practice creating lists, accessing elements with indexing, checking membership, and modifying list elements.
hints: Middle element is at index len(items) // 2
Use tuple unpacking to swap: a, b = b, a
Use in operator to check membership
Check if index is within valid range: -len <= index < len

testCases.description: get_middle_element should return the middle element of an odd-length list
get_middle_element should return None for an empty list
testCases.validation: assert get_middle_element([]) is None, 'get_middle_element([]) should return None'

testCases.description: swap_first_and_last should swap the first and last elements
contains_duplicate should return True when duplicates exist
testCases.validation: assert contains_duplicate([1, 2, 3, 2]) == True, 'contains_duplicate([1,2,3,2]) should return True'

testCases.description: contains_duplicate should return False when all elements are unique
testCases.validation: assert contains_duplicate([1, 2, 3, 4]) == False, 'contains_duplicate([1,2,3,4]) should return False'

testCases.description: safe_get should return default when index is out of range
testCases.validation: assert safe_get([1, 2, 3], 10, 'missing') == 'missing', 'safe_get with out-of-range index should return default'

description: Practice fundamental list operations in Python including indexing to find middle elements, tuple unpacking to swap positions, membership checking with the in operator to detect duplicates, and safe element access with bounds checking. These building blocks are essential for working with sequential data.
